context.story_engine: Multiple noble families vie for power and survival across the Seven Kingdoms while ancient threats emerge from beyond the Wall and across the sea.

plotlines.name: Ned: Honor
plotlines.goal: serve as Hand of the King and protect the realm
plotlines.obstacle: Lannister corruption, dangerous secrets, political betrayal
plotlines.stakes: his life, his family's safety, the realm's stability

plotlines.name: Cersei: Power
plotlines.goal: protect her children's claim to the throne
plotlines.obstacle: Ned's investigation, threat of exposure of incest

plotlines.name: Robb: Independence
plotlines.goal: rescue his father and defend the North

plotlines.name: Jon: Belonging
plotlines.goal: find his place and purpose at the Night's Watch
plotlines.obstacle: bastard status, conflicting loyalties, supernatural threats

plotlines.name: Tyrion: Survival
plotlines.goal: survive false accusations and family conflicts
plotlines.obstacle: Stark accusations, trial by combat, family expectations

plotlines.name: Daenerys: Transformation
plotlines.goal: evolve from pawn to powerful leader
plotlines.obstacle: abusive brother, cultural barriers, magical consequences
plotlines.stakes: her identity, her unborn child, her future

plotlines.name: Joffrey: Succession
plotlines.goal: secure his claim to the Iron Throne

plotlines.name: Bran: Recovery
plotlines.goal: adapt to life as a cripple and find new purpose
plotlines.stakes: his identity, his future role in the world

events.event: Night's Watch soldiers encounter White Walkers who kill all but one
The lone survivor flees south but is captured near Winterfell
Ned executes the deserter for abandoning his post
The Stark family discovers six orphaned direwolf pups
Ned allows each child to adopt a direwolf pup
Jon Arryn dies suddenly under mysterious circumstances
King Robert decides to travel north to Winterfell with his court
Robert arrives at Winterfell and asks Ned to become Hand of the King
Robert proposes to betroth Prince Joffrey to Sansa Stark
Catelyn receives a secret letter from Lysa claiming Lannisters murdered Jon Arryn
Catelyn fears the Lannisters are plotting against King Robert
Bran climbs the walls of Winterfell's tallest tower despite warnings
Bran witnesses Cersei and Jaime engaged in incestuous intercourse
Jaime pushes Bran from the window to protect their secret
Viserys arranges for Daenerys to marry Khal Drogo
Viserys believes this alliance will provide him with an army to reclaim the Iron Throne
Daenerys receives three ancient dragon eggs as a wedding gift

episodes.theme: the price of power and the burden of duty

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore how duty and ambition conflict with personal desires
Bran's discovery of the Lannister secret directly threatens both Ned's investigation and Cersei's position
the audience knows the Lannisters' secret that will drive the conflict between these houses

events.event: Ned departs Winterfell for King's Landing with Sansa and Arya
Catelyn remains at Winterfell to care for unconscious Bran
An assassin with a Valyrian steel dagger attempts to kill Bran
Catelyn fights off the assassin despite suffering cuts to her hands
Bran's direwolf Summer kills the assassin
Catelyn becomes convinced House Lannister orchestrated the assassination attempt
Jon Snow begins his journey north to the Wall with Benjen Stark
Tyrion decides to travel with them to see the Wall
Joffrey encounters Arya practicing sword work with Mycah
Joffrey draws his sword and threatens Mycah
Arya defends Mycah by hitting Joffrey with a stick
Nymeria attacks and bites Joffrey's arm when he threatens Arya
Arya throws Joffrey's sword into the river and flees with Mycah
Arya releases Nymeria into the wild to protect her
Robert holds court to judge the incident with Cersei demanding justice
Sansa refuses to contradict Joffrey's lies, claiming she cannot remember
Cersei demands Lady be executed when Nymeria cannot be found
Robert orders Lady's killing against Ned's protests
Ned personally executes Lady to spare her a crueler death
The royal party discovers Joffrey's men have killed Mycah
Daenerys continues adapting to life as Drogo's wife among the Dothraki
Ser Jorah Mormont guides Daenerys in Dothraki customs

episodes.theme: the cost of honor in a corrupt world

interactions.description: both plotlines explore the conflict between doing what's right and political survival
all three plotlines intersect in the royal court scene where Lady is condemned
both Daenerys and Jon are adapting to new roles and finding their place in unfamiliar worlds

events.event: Ned attends his first Small Council meeting and discovers the realm is deeply in debt
The Small Council discusses financial matters revealing the crown's precarious position
Ned learns Arya has received a sword from Jon and wants to learn swordwork
Ned arranges private sword lessons for Arya with a Braavosi water dancing master
Catelyn travels secretly to King's Landing to warn Ned about Bran's assassination attempt
Littlefinger intercepts Catelyn's arrival and takes her to a brothel to speak privately
Bran awakens from his coma but learns he will never walk again
Bran cannot remember the circumstances of his fall
Jon demonstrates superior fighting skills during Night's Watch training
Jon's abilities create tension with fellow recruits who resent his noble upbringing
Lord Commander Mormont recognizes Jon's potential
Mormont asks Tyrion to speak with King Robert about sending more recruits to the Night's Watch
Daenerys has grown more confident as Khaleesi and is pregnant with Drogo's child
Viserys threatens and abuses Daenerys as he has done throughout their lives
Daenerys stands up to Viserys for the first time, threatening to have him killed if he strikes her again

episodes.theme: the awakening of power and responsibility

interactions.description: all three plotlines show characters discovering their true capabilities and responsibilities
the audience knows the Lannisters pushed Bran, but Bran cannot remember and Ned doesn't know
Tyrion's mission to recruit for the Night's Watch connects his survival arc with Ned's duty as Hand

events.event: Tyrion arrives at Winterfell and presents Bran with blueprints for a special saddle
Maester Luwin examines the saddle design and confirms it will work
Bran gains hope for the first time since his fall
Tyrion departs for King's Landing but stops at an inn at the crossroads
Catelyn unexpectedly appears at the same inn
Catelyn publicly accuses Tyrion of providing the catspaw assassin with the Valyrian steel dagger
Catelyn calls upon her father's bannermen who arrest Tyrion despite his protests
Ned visits the armorer Tobho Mott to investigate Jon Arryn's death
Tobho Mott reveals Jon Arryn came to see Gendry, one of Robert's bastards
Ned observes Gendry has Baratheon features unlike the golden-haired royal children
Samwell Tarly arrives as a new recruit to the Night's Watch
Other trainees brutally beat Sam during training due to his cowardice
Jon intervenes and threatens the recruits with Ghost to stop the abuse
Jon takes Sam under his protection and helps him find ways to contribute
Viserys grows frustrated with his diminished status as Daenerys gains authority
Viserys confronts Daenerys demanding she remember he is the rightful king
Daenerys no longer fears her brother and asserts her independence from his control

episodes.theme: the price of truth and the courage to act on it

interactions.description: all plotlines explore characters taking decisive action despite personal risk—Ned pursuing dangerous truth, Catelyn acting on suspicion, Jon protecting the weak, Daenerys defying her brother
Ned discovers evidence of Joffrey's illegitimacy while the audience knows Cersei's secret
Catelyn's arrest of Tyrion directly impacts both the Stark investigation and Tyrion's survival

events.event: Varys informs King Robert that Daenerys is pregnant with Khal Drogo's child
Robert orders Daenerys's assassination, viewing the unborn child as a threat
Ned refuses to sanction the murder of a pregnant girl and argues Daenerys poses no immediate danger
Ned resigns as Hand of the King in protest and prepares to return to Winterfell
Catelyn arrives at the Eyrie with prisoner Tyrion Lannister
Catelyn presents Tyrion to Lysa Arryn and accuses him of attempting to murder Bran
Lysa agrees to hold Tyrion for trial
News of Tyrion's capture reaches King's Landing, enraging the Lannister family
Jaime confronts Ned in the streets, demanding his brother's immediate release
Ned explains he had no part in Tyrion's capture, but Jaime refuses to believe him
Jaime draws his sword and attacks Ned, escalating to violence
One of Jaime's soldiers stabs Ned in the leg from behind, ending the fight dishonorably
Jaime expresses disgust at his man's interference and declares intention to ride north
Ned collapses wounded as relations between Stark and Lannister completely break down

episodes.theme: the cost of moral principles in a corrupt world

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore the conflict between doing what's right versus what's expedient for survival
Ned's moral stand against assassination directly leads to Jaime's violent confrontation
Tyrion's capture by Catelyn triggers Jaime's demand for his release and violent response

events.event: King Robert reappoints Ned as Hand before departing on a hunting expedition
Reports arrive that Ser Gregor Clegane has committed atrocities in the Riverlands
Ned sentences the Mountain to death and summons Tywin Lannister to court
Ned sends Arya and Sansa back to Winterfell for protection
Ned discovers Joffrey and his siblings are Jaime's children, not Robert's
Bran tests Tyrion's special saddle design
Wildlings attack Bran during his ride
Robb and Theon rescue Bran, kill male wildling and capture Osha
Tyrion demands trial by combat at the Eyrie
Lady Lysa chooses Ser Vardis Egen as her champion
Bronn volunteers to fight as Tyrion's champion for gold
Bronn defeats and kills Ser Vardis, securing Tyrion's freedom
Viserys grows impatient with Drogo's delay of the Westeros invasion
Viserys threatens to kill Daenerys and her unborn child
Drogo executes Viserys by pouring molten gold over his head

episodes.theme: the price of power and the consequences of crossing lines

interactions.description: all three plotlines involve characters facing life-or-death consequences for their choices and principles
Ned's discovery of Joffrey's illegitimacy directly threatens Cersei's power and Joffrey's succession
both Tyrion and Daenerys face mortal threats that ultimately strengthen their positions

events.event: Ned confronts Cersei in the godswood, revealing he knows her children were fathered by Jaime
Ned warns Cersei to flee King's Landing with her children before he tells Robert the truth
King Robert returns from hunting mortally wounded by a boar and summons Ned to his deathbed
Robert dictates his final will, naming Ned as Lord Protector and regent until Prince Joffrey comes of age
Ned deliberately writes 'my rightful heir' instead of Joffrey's name in the will
Ned sends a message to Stannis Baratheon, Robert's brother and the true heir
Ned presents Robert's will to the court and declares Joffrey has no claim to the throne
Cersei tears up the document and orders Ned's arrest for treason
Littlefinger betrays Ned at the crucial moment despite their arrangement
The gold cloaks turn on Ned's men, killing several and capturing Ned
First Ranger Benjen Stark leads a ranging party beyond the Wall but fails to return
Lord Commander Mormont assigns new recruits to their orders within the Night's Watch
Jon receives appointment as Mormont's personal steward rather than a ranger
Sam suggests to Jon that Mormont chose him as steward to groom him for future leadership
An assassin disguised as a wine merchant attempts to poison Daenerys
Ser Jorah grows suspicious and stops Daenerys from drinking the poisoned wine
The assassin confesses under interrogation that King Robert hired him to kill Daenerys
Khal Drogo declares he will cross the narrow sea and conquer the Seven Kingdoms for his unborn son
Drogo orders his khalasar to march toward the coast to begin their invasion of Westeros

episodes.theme: the price of honor in a corrupt world

interactions.description: all plotlines explore the tension between doing what's right and surviving in a hostile environment
Ned's confrontation with Cersei directly triggers the power struggle over Joffrey's legitimacy
Robert's assassination attempt on Daenerys ironically triggers the very invasion he feared

events.event: Syrio Forel fights off Lannister guards to help Arya escape
Sansa Stark is captured by Lannister forces
News of Ned's imprisonment reaches Winterfell
Robb calls the Northern banners and prepares for war
Robb leaves Bran to rule Winterfell and marches south
Catelyn travels to the Eyrie seeking military support
Lysa Arryn refuses to commit the Vale's forces
Catelyn leaves to join Robb's war camp
Tyrion and Bronn are ambushed by Hill Tribes
Tyrion convinces the tribesmen to spare them by promising rewards
Tywin meets with Shagga and offers payment for tribal service
Joffrey appoints Tywin as new Hand of the King
Sansa pleads with Joffrey to show mercy to Ned
Joffrey agrees to spare Ned if he confesses treason
Jon and Sam retrieve corpses of dead rangers
One corpse reanimates as a wight and attacks Mormont
Jon destroys the wight with fire
Drogo's khalasar raids settlements to capture slaves
Drogo sustains a wound while fighting but dismisses it

episodes.theme: the price of power and the consequences of choices

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore the cost of standing by one's principles versus political survival
Tyrion's alliance with Hill Tribes directly serves Tywin's war against the Starks
both Jon and Daenerys face supernatural threats that test their evolving identities

events.event: The Stark army arrives at the Twins
Catelyn negotiates with Lord Walder Frey for passage and military support
Frey demands Robb marry one of his daughters and Arya marry one of his sons
Catelyn accepts Frey's marriage terms to secure the alliance
Lord Commander Mormont presents Jon with Longclaw as reward for saving his life
Khal Drogo's infected wound worsens and he falls into fever near death
Daenerys asks Mirri Maz Duur to perform blood magic to heal Drogo
Mirri warns that only death can pay for life and instructs everyone to stay outside
Daenerys goes into premature labor as the spell begins
Jorah carries Daenerys into the tent despite Mirri's warnings
Robb executes his battle plan, sacrificing two thousand men as a diversion
Robb leads his main force around to capture Jaime Lannister
Robb successfully takes Jaime prisoner
Ned appears before the Great Sept of Baelor for public confession
Ned falsely confesses to treason and declares Joffrey the rightful king
Joffrey ignores the agreement and orders Ned's immediate execution
Sansa, Cersei, and advisors protest Joffrey's decision
Joffrey commands Ser Ilyn Payne to behead Ned
The execution proceeds, shocking the crowd and nobles

episodes.theme: the price of honor in a world without mercy

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore sacrifice—Ned sacrifices his honor for his family, Daenerys risks everything for Drogo, Robb sacrifices men for victory
Ned's execution serves Joffrey's assertion of power while ending Cersei's immediate threat
Robb wins his battle while his father dies, unaware that his victory comes at the cost of losing what he fought to save

events.event: The North declares independence and crowns Robb Stark as King in the North
Stannis and Renly Baratheon each stake claims to the Iron Throne
Tywin Lannister appoints Tyrion as acting Hand of the King
Jon Snow attempts to abandon his Night's Watch vows to join Robb's war
Jon's fellow brothers intervene and convince him to honor his oath
Lord Commander Mormont assigns Jon to expedition beyond the Wall to search for Benjen
Yoren disguises Arya as a boy and smuggles her out of King's Landing
Joffrey announces his intention to make Sansa his queen despite executing her father
Daenerys's child is born deformed and stillborn from Mirri's blood magic
Drogo survives but remains in a vegetative state
Daenerys smothers Drogo to end his suffering
Daenerys places dragon eggs on Drogo's pyre and ties Mirri to the flames
Daenerys walks into the fire despite Jorah's pleas
Daenerys emerges unharmed with three newly-hatched dragons
Jorah and remaining Dothraki kneel before Daenerys

interactions.description: all three plotlines involve characters choosing their true identity and purpose through sacrifice
Lannister power moves directly respond to threats to Joffrey's succession
both Daenerys and Jon face tests of loyalty and emerge with clearer purpose
